Okay, so as we're saying, I was thinking about, okay, everyone probably loves this shit online. This will go viral now because the disaster goes viral. Okay, people fucking, they always say good news travels fast. Bad news travels instantly, right? All right. So anyway, as I was saying, logical certainty is basically a left-brain's logic. It's about value proposition, cost-benefit ratio, whether a product empirically meets someone's needs. That's what logical certainty is all about. And it's a function. It's logic, right? And the way that we create logical certainty typically is through the words that we say. We make what's called an airtight logical case. Now, with the straight line system, we engage in something called strategic preparation. What I mean by that is that since we know that we have to get our prospect to a very high level of logical certainty, how well let's, let me get this thing actually stopped by the way. Let me try it red today. Okay. So let's watch this certainty scale. One represents the state of absolute uncertainty. I mean, they hate your product. I think it sucks, right? Worth a piece of shit in the world. They didn't want to look at just this awful, right? It doesn't meet their needs. It's overpriced. It looks kind of shoddy workmanship on it, right? It's not going to have a positive cost-benefit ratio, right? Not a good match for them. It's worse than the competition. That's a one, biggest piece of shit in the world. And then you have what's called a 10. That means they are absolutely, positively certain that it's the best thing since sliced bread. They love it. It meets all their needs. It makes sense to them when they just think, you know, there's logical certainty. When you're logically certain about something, it means that you can connect all the dots in your mind. You can sort of say, you know, from start to finish, the whole thing makes sense to me. And you can then therefore explain it to someone else. When you're logically certain about something, you can explain to someone else why it makes sense to you very easily. That's very different than emotional certainty. Emotional certainty is a gut feeling, which says, you know, I just, I just want this. I have to have this. It's going to, it's going to make me feel better. That's emotional certainty. It's visceral to your gut. It's not about adding up all the features and benefits. It's not about value proposition. It's mostly about this. Someone imagining themselves in the future using your product and feeling good. In other words, people typically buy things because they are feeling some sort of pain, discomfort, uncomfortability, right? Out of control in some way. They can't get there. There's some part of their life is not in control. And they buy some, they spend their hard-earned money, they purchase something to get back into control, or to resolve their pain, or to fill that void or lack or that need, so to speak, right? To resolve a need. That's basically why people buy stuff. Okay? So emotional certainty is when they save themselves, I see myself six months in the future using your product and feeling great. For a car salesman, it's like, imagine yourself driving down the road with the top down, the wind in your hair, you feel like a million bucks, sexy, great, gorgeous girl sitting next to you, just cruising down, just like exhilarating with that rumbling engine. That's emotional certainty for selling a car. Logical certainty is the value of this car, all the other competing cars from the other, they are more expensive or these has more features and benefits. So it's more like, ABCD versus just imagine yourself in this car feeling awesome. So those are your two types of certainty, right? We have our logical certainty, which is generated by words. And then we have emotional certainty, right? Over here are our emotional certainty, which is generated with two things. One is called future pacing. And that means, as I just said, someone imagines themselves in the future, at some point down the road having made the decision to buy your product, and they are now using it and feeling good. That's emotional certainty, right? And the second way that we create this, which is the one I really want to focus on today, because it's almost like a hack for creating emotional certainty. It's a sales hack, so to speak. I always hate the thing, it's a fucking life hack, but it kind of is a shortcut to creating emotional certainty. And the way we do that is using tonal. There's a certain tonal strategy that you may have heard me talk about before. It's called pace pace lead. Now you might have been taught this by someone else, but here's the thing. There's pace pace lead, and there's pace pace fucking lead ninja style on steroids. That's the way I teach it. And here's the deal. It doesn't fucking work the other way. Pace pace lead is a waste of fucking time. Pace pace fucking lead works and gets you where you want to go. Now what's the difference between two? That's what this training is all about. Remember, sales at the highest level is about the transference of emotion. And the primary emotion to transfer is certainty. That's what's happening, right? In other words, when you walk into a sales setting, you're in a salesman, right? When you walk in, your prospect is going to be typically at a much lower level of certainty than you. You're at a very high level of certainty. You love your product, you know what makes sense, you're excited about it, you know all the features and benefits, you know, you know your product. So watch, you're at this really imaginary, like this is you, you're at this ultra high level of certainty, right? And your prospect is at a much lower level of certainty. They're at a low level of certainty because they don't know everything about your product. They don't know how great it is. They don't have all the benefits in their mind. They don't understand how much it surpasses the competition, right? They don't know what you know. So your job as a sales person is an influencer. The same thing goes for a negotiation, anything, any type of situation of influence. So essentially, you know that what you have, your position is right and your vision of the future is awesome. It's great, right? And the people who try to influence it, someone not as sure as you. So your job essentially as an influencer is to make them as certain as you are. You're transferring your certainty to them and essentially raising up their levels to that same ultra high level as you. That's what's going on when you sell. So as you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, right? You are increasing their level of certainty to match yours. And the way you do that is through a combination of words and tonality. That's what you think. That's what's happening, right? Now remember, it's not just certainty about the product. With the straight line, we always have this policy of first things first. The first thing we got to do is we got to create emotional certainty, right? Why? Because that then frees up the prospect to be moved emotionally, right? You need to create both logical and emotional certainty. One without the other, you can't close it at a very high level. But logic comes first because logic, once you create the logical case, it's very easy to move some emotion. Let me tell you what that sounds like. When I create an airtight logical case, the person, the prospect is saying to us, oh, man, yeah, I love that product. I'm not going to buy it right now. But whoa, that's a great product, man. Oh, boy, wow, that's got some great value there. Oh, I'm not going to buy it yet. Who is it? They love the product, but they're not ready to buy. They're not emotionally attached enough to make a decision. You get it? So when I have my prospect there, they're saying, damn, that's fucking really awesome, right? I'm not going to buy. I'm halfway home. I made massive progress. So the purpose of my initial presentation here is to try to get them as close as I can to, oh, man, that's a great product. But, you know, I'm going to buy that, man, it's awful. Very often, you're not there yet, though. When you make that initial presentation, they might say, oh, that's a pretty good product. That's six or seven. They're not like, damn, that's great. So what happens is my first pattern here, my first loop, is designed to loop back into presentation mode. And now what I'm going to do is I have my airtight logical frame. And now I'm going to fill in my frame with all the beautiful accoutrements and all the benefits. And they say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. That's a six, right? And now I say, exactly, it really is a great idea. In fact, let me say this, the true beauty of the program here, that I loop back, and now comes my secondary presentation, number two. So I've already prepared a secondary presentation, and that presentation is logic based again. In other words, this secondary presentation is designed to fill in the blanks of my frame, my airtight logical frame, and make it like so incredibly over the top, scream from the hilltops logical at the cross, but to say, damn, that's fucking one great cross. I'm not going to buy it right now. Damn, that makes sense. Now I get it. Yeah, I see how it's just much better than the competition. The value proposition is awesome. It's got the best speech out here. The benefits and fries are unbelievable. Yeah, wow. Boy, I'm excited. That's what you want. So the purpose of your follow up presentation is to do precisely that. You're essentially picking up where your airtight logical frame left off, and now it's like you frame a house. Now you put the drywall, the electric, right, you're filling in the blanks to make it beautiful and irresistible on a logical level. So that's what you're doing in this second case, right? And then what happens is eventually you go through your hole in the toilet, you'll ask for the order again, right? But here's the deal. But here's the hack. We are going to have to create the emotional case, because in the absence of emotion, they're going to say, let me think about it. That's what happens. So when someone is logically certain, but not emotionally certain, they're like, oh, wow, it sounds really good. Let me think about it. Let me quickly get back. Let me do some research. restaurant, listen, here's the thing, it's, you know, to have an accent is not problematic, so long as your English is proficient, you know, the key with Russian is that they don't have definite articles, so you'll typically say I want to go to store versus the store, I have a friend, my friend Ilya, okay, he's an accountant, a very, very well respected accountant, he moved here, he was 20 years old, and he still has a Russian accent, but his English is impeccable, so it's irrelevant that he has an accent, okay, because his English is impeccable, and this is not, I know this people call me from all over, so it's not just in English, what's the native language, it's more about speaking well than the acting, you can have a Southern accent, you can have a New York accent, an ethnic accent, it doesn't really matter, but does your, is your English proficient, or are you dropping words and making that, that's, you know, so it's a psych-scod, that's pretty fucking tough when you're on the phone with someone, thanks God, you know, good luck, right, so listen, here's what I always say, do your best, best you can, that might mean taking speech lessons, seriously, learning how to minimize your accent, and you can do that, I always say if I move to a foreign country, you know what the first thing I would do is I become proficient, I become a master of their language, if I was going to live in Italy, I'd fucking learn Italian as good as any Italian, I would never let language hold me, you know, hold me back, be a deficit, now that means that we work to it, it might take me six months to really become proficient, but I would do it, I would study my fucking ass off, I would never be a person, like, it's very common from Latin American, when they come up here, right, they come up to the United States, and you know, when they come legally or illegally, but I think it's mostly the ones that come illegally, because they somehow feel like they hide in the shadows, because the legal ones will typically embrace and learn the language, they're going to school, right, but even if you came here illegally, right, which you shouldn't, right, the point is you got to learn the Listen, the words, the words are designed to build logical certainty, your first mileage pan is not a loop, it's your presentation, so your presentation is about logical certainty, your first loop is about filling in the gaps to make it an airtight logical case, your second loop has some logic to button up the certainty, but then you switch to future pacing, and after that you're almost exclusively on emotion, but you always want to button up a little bit of logic, that's the key, just remember this in closing here, the key to closing at the highest level is you must make the person both logically and emotionally certain, one without the other does not work very well, remember in the absence of logical certainty, you know what happens, the person's like says bullshit, bullshit, the logical mind serves as a human bullshit detector, that's what it's there for, to essentially protect this, I'm gonna put this here so you can think, I don't know, so again the logical mind serves as the human bullshit detector, it's designed to stop you from getting carried away by hyping bullshit, that's what's happening, so if you don't get someone extremely certain on a logical level, as they start to be moved emotionally, their brain says bullshit and holds them back, however, once you create that airtight logical case, a really extreme from the hilltops airtight logical case, it then frees them up to be moved emotionally very easily, I want my prospect to be saying wow, that's really great this product, it makes a lot of sense, I'm not buying it yet, who is that a great product, that's logical certainty, that emotion is certain, you get it, so then what happens is once I have them there, then for a process of pace, pace lead and future pacing, I then move them emotionally, that's the key and that will allow you to close at the absolute highest level, I promise you, logic first, emotion second, that's the ticket, all right, and that I will see you guys next week, my gift to you this week, you'll be getting my daily motivations for five days, hope you all enjoyed them and hopefully you'll slide back to that, take care, I love you all and see you next
